definition: UCIM is the framework Jensen runs on. A living knowledge genome that ingests signals from documents, conversations, tool integrations and decision history, then organizes them into a contextual graph that evolves with use. Concepts are reinforced when they prove useful and decay when they fall out of relevance, so the matrix stays current without manual curation.
matters: It separates Sarif from firms that claim "AI-native methodology" but actually run static prompts. UCIM is the technical substrate that makes continuous metabolism possible. It is also the quiet assertion that Sarif owns proprietary intelligence infrastructure.
appears: About page (brief mention). Methodology essays (primary). Lexicon cross-references in Praxis posts. Internal workstation documentation.
